items.title: Supervised learning
items.description: Supervised learning requires labeled targets y and optimizes prediction error; unsupervised learning discovers structure in X alone without predefined labels.

items.title: Self-supervised learning
items.description: Self-supervised methods create surrogate labels from the data (e.g. masked prediction). Unsupervised learning here means classical structure discovery without such constructed targets.

items.title: k-Means clustering
items.description: k-means is a canonical clustering algorithm under the unsupervised paradigm: it partitions points into k groups by minimizing within-cluster variance.

items.title: Principal Component Analysis
items.description: PCA is a foundational dimensionality reduction method that finds orthogonal directions of maximum variance—an unsupervised way to summarize high-dimensional data.
